summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Semester System and Portal Email features. All 18 backend tests passed. All frontend UI features verified working correctly.

minor.issue: Resend API in test mode - can only send to verified email (onboarding@resend.dev sender). Returns 520 timeout for non-verified recipients. This is expected behavior for testing environment.

issues: Minor React hydration warnings in console for table elements (span inside th/td/tr/tbody) - does not affect functionality

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/semesters returns list of semesters with correct structure
Backend: GET /api/semesters/active returns active or most recent semester
Backend: POST /api/semesters creates new semester (admin only)
Backend: PUT /api/semesters/{id} updates semester
Backend: Toggle semester active status (only one active at a time)
Backend: DELETE /api/semesters/{id} deletes semester
Backend: DELETE semester with classes fails with 400
Backend: Gradebook endpoint exists and returns classes
Backend: Semesters available for gradebook filtering
Backend: Gradebook report endpoint works
Backend: Portal token generation includes expiration date
Backend: Portal email endpoint exists and processes requests
Backend: Portal email accepts different expiration days (7, 14, 30, 60, 90)
Backend: Portal token expiration validation
Backend: Portal email requires student_id (422 validation)
Backend: Portal email requires valid email format (422 validation)
Backend: Portal email with invalid student returns 404
Backend: Semester create requires admin authentication (401)
Frontend: Admin Panel has Semesters tab
Frontend: Semesters tab shows semester list with name, dates, year term
Frontend: New Semester button present (data-testid='new-semester-btn')
Frontend: Semester active toggle switch present
Frontend: Edit and Delete buttons for semesters
Frontend: Gradebook shows semester selector (data-testid='gradebook-semester-select')
Frontend: Gradebook shows 'Trabajando en: [Semester]' indicator when selected
Frontend: Gradebook semester dropdown shows all semesters with active indicator
Frontend: Gradebook Reports has semester filter (data-testid='report-semester-select')
Frontend: Parent portal link dialog shows expiration date (Expira: date)
Frontend: Portal dialog has parent email input field (data-testid='portal-parent-email')
Frontend: Portal dialog has expiration days selector (7, 14, 30, 60, 90 days)
Frontend: Send Email button present (data-testid='send-portal-email-btn')
Frontend: Portal tokens have expiration dates displayed
critical_code_review_comments: React hydration warnings in Gradebook.js table - span elements being inserted inside table elements. Not critical but should be addressed for cleaner console output.
seed_data_creation: Restored Semester 2 (sem_ba17a81e) as active semester. Portal token: portal_4381b9aa67ef461ba879a755905bf0b7 with expiration 2026-03-14
context_for_next_testing_agent: Semester system and Portal Email features fully functional. Two semesters exist: Semester 1 (inactive) and Semester 2 (active). Portal email uses Resend API (test mode - onboarding@resend.dev sender). Test credentials: test@school.edu / testpassword (role: super_admin). Key data-testid attributes: new-semester-btn, gradebook-semester-select, report-semester-select, portal-parent-email, portal-expire-days, send-portal-email-btn

semester_system.admin_panel_tab: Semesters tab in Admin Panel with CRUD operations
semester_system.semester_fields: name, name_es, start_date, end_date, year_term, is_active
semester_system.active_toggle: Only one semester can be active at a time
semester_system.gradebook_selector: Semester dropdown in Gradebook with 'Trabajando en' indicator

portal_email.dialog_location: Classes page > Student row > Green link icon
portal_email.email_input: Parent email input field with data-testid='portal-parent-email'
portal_email.resend_integration: Uses Resend API (test mode with onboarding@resend.dev sender)

notes: Resend API is in test mode - emails can only be sent to verified email addresses. In production, a verified domain would be used. The portal email endpoint works correctly but returns errors for non-verified recipients.
